zh.lifeSummary: 當Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres於1410年7月14日出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德艾姆-賓時，他的父親Jan II Lord of Egmond25歲，母親Maria van Arkel22歲。他於1430年1月26日與Catherine von Cleves在德國海因里希_海普洛文次可列福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1473年2月23日在荷蘭努-布拉班特格拉福享年62歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-布拉班特格拉福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres于1410年7月14日出生在荷兰努-拉藍德艾姆-賓时，他的父亲Jan II Lord of Egmond25岁，母亲Maria van Arkel22岁。他于1430年1月26日与Catherine von Cleves在德国海因里希_海普洛文次可列福结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1473年2月23日在荷兰努-布拉班特格拉福享年62岁，埋葬在荷兰努-布拉班特格拉福。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Egmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Monique, Amie, Andre, Cecile, Desire, Dumas, Jacques, Lucienne, Luckner, Napoleon, Patrice.

Scottish, English, French, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Edmond (Old English Ēadmund, composed of the elements ēad ‘prosperity, fortune’ + mund ‘protection’). It is sometimes found as Admond, where the vowel has been shortened. The personal name was still well used in England in the late 14th century, especially in East Anglia, and was often bestowed in honor of the East Anglian King Saint Edmund the Martyr (died 869), who was killed by pagan Danish invaders.

English: occasionally a variant either of Edman or Eman, the latter from an alternative form of the Middle English female personal name Emeny, arising from Ismaine. The origin of Ismaine is uncertain, though possibly ancient Germanic from an unrecorded Ismagin, perhaps from īs ‘ice’ or īsen- ‘iron’ + -magin ‘strength’, though -magin is not known as a second element. Occasionally Eman may also be a variant of Emond .

variants.name: Edman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Swen, Thor.

English (Lincolnshire): from the Middle English personal name Ed(e)man, occasionally Ad(e)man (Old English Ēadmann, from ēad ‘prosperity’ + mann ‘man’). Alternatively, a variant of Edmond . In Middle English, Edmund (also occasionally found as Admund) was probably sometimes confused with Edman and Adman, and the resulting surnames were almost certainly interchangeable in post-medieval times.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the elements ed ‘isthmus’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Edmiston
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): habitational name from Edmonstone (Midlothian) or Edmonston (Lanarkshire). The former is so named from the Old English personal name Ēadmund (see Edmond ) + tūn ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Edmon
meanings: English: altered form of   Edmond  .

variants.name: Edmonds
meanings: English: variant of Edmond with genitival -s or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Edmondson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Edmond + -son.

variants.name: Edmund
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
English: variant of   Edmond  . This surname is also established in the West Indies.

nameMeanings.name: Duke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English duk(ke), duck, doke, dook ‘duck’ (Old English dūce), either from a perceived resemblance (perhaps a waddling gait) or from association with wild fowling. Compare Duck , Drake .

English: from the Middle English personal name Duk or Duke. In northern England this is usually a pet form of Marmaduke. It may alternatively be a survival of one or more Old English personal names, though it is uncertain whether they were still current in the period of surname formation. Old English Ducc(a) is attested in placenames like Duxford (Cambridgeshire) and Duckington (Cheshire), and was perhaps interchangeable with Docc, attested in Doxey (Staffordshire) and Doxford (Northumberland). Duke could also represent Old English Deowuc (as in Deuxhill, Shropshire). A surname from Marmaduke is on record until at least 1881 and derives from the personal name Marmaduke, apparently an Anglo-Norman French pronunciation of Old Irish Maolmaedóc ‘devotee of Maedóc’; see Duckett .
Americanized form of Polish Duk: nickname from dukać ‘to stammer or falter’.

variants.name: Dock
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Duke  .
Norwegian: habitational name from a farm named Dokk, from Old Norse dǫkk ‘hollow, depression’.
North German (Lower Saxony): possibly a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked in the cloth trade, from Middle Low German dōk ‘fabric’.

variants.name: Doke
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .

variants.name: Duch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Nga, Phal, Phan, Sophan, Tha, Thang, Yen.  German Kurt, Klaus.
Polish, Sorbian, and Ukrainian: nickname from duch ‘spirit, breath’.
Czech: from a short form of the Old Czech personal name Duchoslav, composed of the elements duch ‘spirit’ + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’.

variants.name: Duckett
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Duket. This may sometimes have been an early Middle English pet form of an Old English Ducc(a), but was more often a pet form of Marmaduke, especially in Yorkshire (see Duke ). Marmaduke itself is from Irish Maol Maedóc ‘servant of Maedóc’, Maedóc being the name of prominent Irish saints of the 6th and 7th centuries.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

variants.name: Dukes
meanings: English: variant of Duke , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Arnoud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Arnold .

historicEvents.description: The courtyard Episcopal palace at Atrecht had witch burnings.

en.lifeSummary: When Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres was born on 14 July 1410, in Egmond-Binnen, North Holland, Netherlands, his father, Jan II Lord of Egmond, was 25 and his mother, Maria van Arkel, was 22. He married Catherine von Cleves on 26 January 1430, in Kleve, Rhineland, Prussia, Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 23 February 1473, in Grave, North Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 62, and was buried in Grave, North Brabant, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres est né le 14 juillet 1410, à Egmond-Binnen, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Jan II Lord of Egmond, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Maria van Arkel, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Catherine von Cleves le 26 janvier 1430, à Kleve, Rhénanie, Prusse, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 23 février 1473, à Grave, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Grave, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres am 14. Juli 1410 in Egmond-Binnen, Nord-Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jan II Lord of Egmond 25 und seine Mutter Maria van Arkel 22. Er hat am 26. Januar 1430 in Kleve, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, Deutschland Catherine von Cleves geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Februar 1473 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Grave, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande und wurde in Grave, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres è nato il il 14 luglio 1410 nel Egmond-Binnen, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Jan II Lord of Egmond, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Maria van Arkel, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Catherine von Cleves il 26 gennaio 1430, nel Kleve, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 23 febbraio 1473, nel Grave, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Grave, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldresが1410年7月14日にオランダノオルド-hオランドエグモンド-ビンで生まれた時，父Jan II Lord of Egmondは25歳，母Maria van Arkelは22歳であった。1430年1月26日にドイツプレウssンルヘインプロヴィンズクレブでCatherine von Clevesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1473年2月23日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントグラブで62歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-ブラバントグラブにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres(이)가 1410년 7월 14일 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 그몽-빈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jan II Lord of Egmond(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria van Arkel(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1430년 1월 26일 독일 플레우ss 리인플로빈츠 클브에서 Catherine von Cleves(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1473년 2월 23일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 그레이브에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 그레이브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres nasceu em 14 de julho de 1410, em Egmond-Binnen, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Jan II Lord of Egmond, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Maria van Arkel, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine von Cleves em 26 de janeiro de 1430, em Kleve, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de fevereiro de 1473, em Grave, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Grave, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Arnoud van Egmond Duke de Gueldres родился 14 июля 1410 г. в местности эгмонд-биннен, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Jan II Lord of Egmond было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria van Arkel, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine von Cleves 26 января 1430 г. в местности клив, раинпровинз, Пруссия, Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 23 февраля 1473 г. в местности грав, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности грав, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды.

Se casó con Catherine von Cleves el 26 de enero de 1430, en Kleve, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 23 de febrero de 1473, en Grave, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Grave, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos.
